original_hypothesis.title: Whitelist update can permanently strand user claims by de-whitelisting a version with existing reserves (no migration/withdraw path)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ASDUSDC.withdraw requires the USDC version to be whitelisted. If the owner de-whitelists a version that still has usdcBalances and real reserves, users cannot withdraw that version anymore, effectively locking those reserves unless an out-of-band migration is performed. This is an operational dead-end state for user claims tied to that version’s reserves.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Even if intended as a safety switch, without an explicit migration mechanism it creates a high-severity operational failure mode during upgrades/emergencies.
deployment_assumptions: Owner may de-whitelist a version while usdcBalances[_version] > 0 (e.g., token upgrade, blacklist, pause incident)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No migration/admin withdrawal function exists in ASDUSDC; withdraw() hard-requires whitelist

proof_of_signal.pattern: withdraw() has require(whitelistedUSDCVersions[_usdcVersion]); updateWhitelist can set false; no alternative withdraw/migrate

preconditions: Some users have minted asdUSDC backed by a specific USDC version

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Operational control (whitelist) also gates withdrawals, creating a lock condition

operational_questions: Is de-whitelisting intended to block new deposits only, or also withdrawals?
What emergency/migration runbook exists to unwind a de-whitelisted version’s reserves?
Are there on-chain constraints preventing de-whitelisting when balances exist?
validation_requirements: Test state where usdcBalances[version]>0 then de-whitelist; confirm withdraw reverts
Assess feasibility of adding a migration/escape hatch without breaking invariants

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Accumulate asdUSDC supply backed by a version (normal user behavior)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Trigger or wait for owner de-whitelisting event
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Observe inability to withdraw that version; secondary market discount/insolvency dynamics
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: requires owner action or incident response

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: User funds locked for that version; redemption liquidity crisis
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High (requires contract upgrade/redeploy or special admin migration)
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: asdUSDC withdrawals, router swap pipeline relying on asdUSDC liquidity

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Whitelist toggles to false while usdcBalances non-zero
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Spike in withdraw reverts after whitelist change

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
